title: The One Where They Build A House

jokes.text: He even did so for the prison he built... and, somewhat ironically, would later occupy.
jokes.setup: George Sr. always had ribbon-cutting ceremonies for his construction projects
jokes.punchline: Including for the prison he'd eventually be imprisoned in
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Arrested Development's dense ironic foreshadowing. The word 'somewhat' is brilliantly understated for such perfect irony. Economy is flawless - maximum comedy per word.

jokes.text: That's like, what, like a million diamonds for $400? A million- diamonds.
jokes.setup: Lindsay is excited about diamond cream that costs $400
jokes.punchline: Her math suggests this is somehow a million diamonds for $400
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Lindsay's vapid materialism and terrible math skills. The repetition of 'million- diamonds' shows her trying to convince herself.

jokes.text: Or should I say 'Bluepies'?
jokes.setup: Tobias claims he's been meeting groupies since auditioning for Blue Man Group
jokes.punchline: He coins the portmanteau 'Bluepies' for Blue Man Group groupies
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias - creating awkward portmanteaus while lying about his romantic success. The word itself is inherently funny.

jokes.text: In fact, neither Lindsay nor Tobias did have the guts to go through with it. - I already have. - I have too.
jokes.setup: Narrator states neither has the guts to see other people
jokes.punchline: Both immediately claim they already have
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic structure - narrator contradiction immediately followed by characters proving the point. The timing is impeccable.

jokes.text: Lindsay at bar failing to get anyone's attention while Tobias watches
jokes.setup: Lindsay confidently claimed she's been meeting people
jokes.punchline: Visual of her complete failure to attract anyone
jokes.explanation: Visual payoff to the narrator's revelation - shows rather than tells their incompetence.

jokes.text: Who? Ann. You know, she's- she's the girl I'm kinda hanging out with. - I haven't met Ann. - Yes, you have.
jokes.setup: George Michael wants to bring Ann to the ribbon cutting
jokes.punchline: Michael has no idea who Ann is despite having met her
jokes.explanation: Establishes the running gag of Ann being completely forgettable. Michael's genuine confusion despite having met her is perfect.

jokes.text: Hey, you! She's got a little hard-boiled egg going there.
jokes.setup: Michael finally recognizes Ann
jokes.punchline: He calls out to her as 'Hey, you!' and describes her as having a 'hard-boiled egg going'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual description that's both specific and absurd. 'Hard-boiled egg going there' is perfectly strange phrasing that became iconic.

jokes.text: She calls it a mayonn-egg.
jokes.setup: George Michael describes Ann's eating habits with mayo packets and eggs
jokes.punchline: Ann has coined the portmanteau 'mayonn-egg'
jokes.explanation: Absurd portmanteau that perfectly captures Ann's bland, practical approach to food. The word itself is both logical and ridiculous.

jokes.text: It's Ann. Her na- all your money getting her all glittered up for Easter.
jokes.setup: George Michael corrects Michael that her name is Ann
jokes.punchline: Michael immediately calls her 'Egg' and references Easter
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that extends the egg metaphor to Easter. Michael's interruption and immediate return to 'Egg' shows he's not listening.

jokes.text: all your money getting her all glittered up for Easter.
jokes.setup: Talking about buying Ann diamonds
jokes.punchline: Easter reference combining egg joke with actual holiday
jokes.explanation: Multi-layered joke connecting Ann's egg-like appearance to Easter while dismissing her worth.

jokes.text: Dad may have illegally built some homes in Iraq, which is kinda not cool.
jokes.setup: Discussing the company's serious legal troubles
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement calling war profiteering 'kinda not cool'
jokes.explanation: Perfect understatement humor - treating massive corporate scandal as minor social faux pas.

jokes.text: Paper covers rock. - It is a rock though. It should beat everything.
jokes.setup: Gob loses at rock paper scissors
jokes.punchline: He argues that rock should logically beat everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob logic - refusing to accept the rules of a simple game. His childish reasoning is both absurd and characteristic.

jokes.text: There's not a lot of logic to it. It's like on a boat with 'Women and children first.' Why should they-which, before I forget, I'm buying a company boat.
jokes.setup: Gob continues complaining about rock paper scissors logic
jokes.punchline: He starts to question maritime safety protocols but gets distracted by announcing he's buying a boat
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob tangent that reveals his selfishness and short attention span. The misdirection from misogyny to boat purchase is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Oh, I'll make you a firm offer. Unfortunately, she wasn't flirting.
jokes.setup: Boat saleswoman asks for a 'firm offer'
jokes.punchline: Gob takes it sexually while she means business
jokes.explanation: Simple sexual misunderstanding elevated by the narrator's deadpan clarification. Shows Gob's inability to read social situations.

jokes.text: The Seaward. - You're not getting a boat. Get rid of the Seaward. - I'll leave when I'm good and ready.
jokes.setup: Michael tells Gob to get rid of 'the Seaward' (boat name)
jokes.punchline: Lucille thinks he called her a 'C-word' and responds defensively
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant wordplay that works on multiple levels. The boat name sounds like a profanity, creating perfect misunderstanding. Lucille's dignified response is perfect.

jokes.text: Fine. But rock sinks boat.
jokes.setup: Callback to the rock paper scissors argument
jokes.punchline: Michael applies the logic to Gob's boat purchase
jokes.explanation: Clever callback that extends the earlier rock paper scissors logic to the boat situation. Perfect economy and timing.

jokes.text: Mother? Mother! - I heard zoo noises. - It's nothing. Go back to bed.
jokes.setup: Buster investigates sounds from his mother's room
jokes.punchline: He describes Oscar and Lucille's intimacy as 'zoo noises'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Buster childishness - describing adult sounds with innocent confusion. 'Zoo noises' is both accurate and absurd.

jokes.text: My- Reproductive organ... is shaped like- a lobster tail... but without its shell, hmm?
jokes.setup: Buster lists medical conditions to avoid military service
jokes.punchline: He describes his anatomy with an oddly specific comparison
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and disturbing imagery that's perfectly Buster. The pause and 'hmm?' suggests he's proud of the comparison.

jokes.text: But Buster had miscalculated the army's current need for personnel. Okay, then, let's get you fitted for a uniform.
jokes.setup: Buster lists numerous disqualifying medical conditions
jokes.punchline: The army accepts him anyway due to personnel needs
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - all of Buster's elaborate excuses are worthless because the army is desperate. Shows the absurdity of bureaucracy.

jokes.text: I mean, we're all out of 'prophylacticos' anyway. Somebody used the last two on his feet to walk across the bathroom floor.
jokes.setup: Kitty talks about running out of condoms
jokes.punchline: George Sr. used them as foot protection for the bathroom floor
jokes.explanation: Absurd and gross image that perfectly captures George Sr.'s selfish resourcefulness. The Spanish 'prophylacticos' adds to the comedy.

jokes.text: Gob was hurt. - Bye, Pop. Oh, for God's- I called to talk to Michael, you horse's-
jokes.setup: George Sr. calls and Gob thinks it's for him
jokes.punchline: George Sr. cuts him off mid-insult when he realizes it's Gob
jokes.explanation: Perfect family dysfunction - George Sr. immediately regrets talking to Gob and starts insulting him. The narrator's commentary adds layers.

jokes.text: Portugal. Down South America way.
jokes.setup: Michael asks where their father was calling from
jokes.punchline: Gob thinks Portugal is in South America
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective display of Gob's ignorance. The casual delivery makes it funnier - he's confident in his wrong geography.

jokes.text: Marijuana? No, no. That's my brother. 'Brothero. Brothero.'
jokes.setup: George Sr. is mistaken for Oscar by Mexican police
jokes.punchline: He tries to explain in broken Spanish by saying 'brothero'
jokes.explanation: George Sr.'s attempt at Spanish by adding 'o' to 'brother' is both desperate and ridiculous. Shows his privileged ignorance.

jokes.text: Uh, uh, uh, you get out. Get out. Get out. Out, out, out! No homeless in here! - Homeless? You're homeless? You're gross. Oh, God.
jokes.setup: Lindsay is attracted to a man who looks like a movie star
jokes.punchline: She immediately reverses when learning he's homeless
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lindsay shallowness - immediate physical revulsion based solely on economic status. The repetitive 'out' shows her panic.

jokes.text: Now which pinkie do you use less?
jokes.setup: Buster panics about being drafted
jokes.punchline: Lucille casually suggests cutting off a finger
jokes.explanation: Darkly hilarious maternal advice. Lucille's casual tone about mutilation is perfectly in character. The practical question makes it even funnier.

jokes.text: I said, 'That's why you're no longer president.' Two weeks. Let's do it in two weeks. Hey!
jokes.setup: Michael suggests a two-month timeline
jokes.punchline: Gob cuts it to two weeks while claiming Michael is 'no longer president'
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob incompetence - making unrealistic promises to seem important. The 'Hey!' suggests he's pleased with his own cleverness.

jokes.text: Solid as a rock. Meet Starla, the new Bluth Company business model... for our phase two campaign.
jokes.setup: Gob promises a business model
jokes.punchline: His 'business model' is literally a boat model/saleswoman
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual pun - 'business model' as both a plan and a literal model. Perfectly captures Gob's shallow, appearance-focused thinking.

jokes.text: Lucille Bluth, caught on tape breathing life into a homeless man. The surprising home video at 10:00.
jokes.setup: News reports on Lucille and Oscar's public affection
jokes.punchline: Describes CPR in suggestive terms
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection - what sounds like a sex scandal is actually CPR. 'Breathing life into' is perfectly ambiguous phrasing.

jokes.text: I wonder if he even has an apartment or- Perfect. He's not a real man.
jokes.setup: Lindsay suggests using a homeless man for construction
jokes.punchline: Lucille finds homelessness perfect because 'he's not a real man'
jokes.explanation: Lucille's casual classism delivered with perfect deadpan. The logic is both cruel and absurd - homelessness = not being a real man.

jokes.text: My God, I was beaten up here. I'm telling you. I'm George. I'm not Oscar. I'm George. - The Cornballer. - Ssí. The Cornballer.
jokes.setup: George Sr. tries to prove his identity to Mexican police
jokes.punchline: Mentioning the Cornballer proves his identity
jokes.explanation: Callback to George Sr.'s history of dangerous products. The police recognition suggests the Cornballer's notorious reputation.

jokes.text: Well, at first, I thought you were kind of hot, but I hadn't looked closely enough.
jokes.setup: Lindsay apologizes for her reaction to learning he was homeless
jokes.punchline: She explains she found him attractive before looking closely
jokes.explanation: Lindsay's attempt at apology makes it worse. Her honesty about appearance-based judgment is both cruel and in-character.

jokes.text: I wanna buy Ann some diamond dust. I'm just hoping to get mildly injured, so I can get out of the army.
jokes.setup: Crew members state their motivations
jokes.punchline: George Michael's romantic gesture contrasts with Buster's self-harm plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect character contrast - innocent romance versus calculated self-harm. Buster's casual tone about injury is particularly funny.

jokes.text: Where's your 'P'? What? Oh, no, not again. I got it.
jokes.setup: Starla notices something missing from Gob
jokes.punchline: Gob has lost the 'P' from 'PRESIDENT' on his shirt/sign
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that literally diminishes Gob's authority. 'Not again' suggests this happens regularly, making it a character trait.

jokes.text: Hmm? L- the business model. Actually, they just made out. Yeah, she had all kinds of orgasms.
jokes.setup: Gob brags about his encounter with Starla
jokes.punchline: He awkwardly reveals they only made out but claims multiple orgasms
jokes.explanation: Gob's desperate exaggeration undermines his own story. The contradiction between 'made out' and 'orgasms' shows his insecurity.

jokes.text: I just wanted you to be impressed with me. Dad never was. Never said a nice thing about me.
jokes.setup: Michael and Gob argue about credit and recognition
jokes.punchline: Gob reveals his desperate need for approval
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Gob vulnerability that's both touching and pathetic. Shows the root of his attention-seeking behavior.

jokes.text: A house truly solid as a rock.
jokes.setup: Gob's confident presentation of the house
jokes.punchline: House immediately collapses, proving the opposite
jokes.explanation: Perfect dramatic irony - the timing of the collapse is comedically perfect.

jokes.text: I got the wrong homeless man. I am sorry. I was looking for Lindsay.
jokes.setup: House collapses revealing Tobias and a homeless man inside
jokes.punchline: Tobias matter-of-factly explains the mix-up
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tobias awkwardness - casual explanation of being found with wrong homeless man. Implies he was spying on Lindsay.

jokes.text: I don't agree with your dirty doings here... but I will defend with my life your right to do it.
jokes.setup: Buster sees something he misinterprets in the collapsed house
jokes.punchline: He makes a patriotic speech about defending rights he finds morally objectionable
jokes.explanation: Brilliant parody of military rhetoric applied to imagined sexual situation. Buster's earnest patriotism makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Gob charged at Michael with the scissors... but Michael, as he always did, picked rock- Which beat scissors.
jokes.setup: Gob attacks with scissors during their fight
jokes.punchline: Michael instinctively plays rock paper scissors, which saves him
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant callback that pays off the entire rock paper scissors setup. The logic is both absurd and perfectly constructed. This is peak Arrested Development writing.

jokes.text: That's Ann. Who's- It doesn't matter.
jokes.setup: George Michael points out Ann in the newspaper photo
jokes.punchline: Michael starts to ask about her but immediately gives up
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback to Ann's forgettability. Michael's mid-sentence abandonment of interest is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Did I hang up? I meant to hit speaker. Oh. You gotta hit the line first. I'm hitting both at the same time.
jokes.setup: Michael claims to have impressively hung up on their father
jokes.punchline: He accidentally hung up while trying to use speakerphone
jokes.explanation: Deflates Michael's moment of defiance by revealing it was accidental. The technical incompetence adds to the humor.

jokes.text: Hello? They hung up.
jokes.setup: Phone conversation with dad
jokes.punchline: Gob doesn't realize he hung up on himself
jokes.explanation: Classic Gob technological incompetence played for maximum comedy.

jokes.text: Look. You're a really nice chick, but I'm not homeless. I'm Tom Jane.
jokes.setup: Lindsay approaching Tom Jane again
jokes.punchline: His matter-of-fact revelation of his celebrity status
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor with real actor. His casual delivery of celebrity status is perfectly understated.

jokes.text: Look. You're a really nice chick, but I'm not homeless. I'm Tom Jane.
jokes.setup: Lindsay approaches the homeless man again
jokes.punchline: He reveals he's actually actor Tom Jane
jokes.explanation: Clever celebrity cameo that subverts expectations. The casual delivery of his real name contrasts with the buildup.

jokes.text: You're really sweet, but, no. I'm Tom Jane.
jokes.setup: Lindsay asks Tom Jane to buy her a drink
jokes.punchline: He rejects her using his celebrity status
jokes.explanation: Extends the Tom Jane reveal joke. His name-dropping as rejection is perfectly Hollywood.

jokes.text: Oh, yes, well, I ran out of blue, and I couldn't find anything else. I think my nipples are bleeding though. I don't know if you're supposed to use quite this much.
jokes.setup: Lindsay notices George Michael's diamond cream on Tobias
jokes.punchline: Tobias used it as blue body paint and is suffering physical consequences
jokes.explanation: Combines multiple episode elements - Blue Man Group obsession and diamond cream. Tobias's casual mention of bleeding nipples is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: I think I need to go to the hospital right away and see if I can't get some of this diamond dust vacuumed from out of my lungs.
jokes.setup: Tobias used diamond cream as body paint
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's been inhaling expensive diamonds and needs medical attention
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the diamond cream joke to its logical absurd conclusion.

tmdb_overview: Michael is driven crazy by Gob when he impresses the board as the new company figurehead due to Michael's legal troubles. Meanwhile, George Sr. is arrested in Mexico for a crime Oscar committed, and Buster witnesses something that utterly shocks him.
insight: Housing construction montage sustains 88.9 index through character-driven irony, not punchlines.
